channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Considering Taxpayer Contributions in Aboriginal Title Claims: The Cowichan Case
item.description: In August 2025, Justice Barbara Young of the British Columbia Supreme Court ruled that the Cowichan Tribes hold Aboriginal title to 800 acres in Richmond, B.C., including government lands and 150 priv...

item.title: ASX lags behind global rally despite strong gains in international markets
item.description: Global equity markets are rebounding strongly despite ongoing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East and a sharp rise in oil prices, with Brent crude surpassing $US106 per barrel. Investors in major...

item.title: Editorial cartoons capture the week’s key political moments and controversies
item.description: The article presents a curated gallery of political cartoons that reflect the major events and tensions in U.S. politics over the past week. Every week, political cartoonists from across the country u...

item.title: Trump responds to Prince Harry's Ukraine comments before upcoming royal visit
item.description: Former U.S. President Donald Trump has publicly downplayed recent comments made by Prince Harry regarding the situation in Ukraine, stating that he does not view the remarks as significant. This excha...

item.title: Putin defends internet shutdowns in Russia as a security measure
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin has defended the recent internet outages in Russia, attributing them to security measures aimed at protecting the country from potential threats. Speaking at a governm...

item.title: Cazzie David is single in 2026 and focusing on her writing and film career
item.description: Cazzie David, the 31‑year‑old American writer and comedian, is single in 2026 and has no publicly confirmed boyfriend at this time. Her last known serious relationship was with graphic designer Miller...

item.title: Fazil Razak’s 'Moham' wins Best Film at Moscow International Film Festival 2026
item.description: Director Fazil Razak’s film, 'Moham', has won the prestigious Best Film award at the Moscow International Film Festival 2026. The film, which marks Razak’s sophomore venture, tells the story of Amala,...

item.title: Man Faces Second-Degree Murder Charge in New Waterford Death
item.description: A man has been arrested in connection with a suspicious death in New Waterford, Nova Scotia. The 70-year-old victim was found dead after a serious incident, prompting a police investigation. Authoriti...

item.title: Beverley Callard unable to attend I’m a Celebrity final due to medical advice following cancer diagnosis
item.description: Beverley Callard has revealed she is deeply disappointed after being advised by doctors not to attend the live final of I’m a Celebrity: South Africa. The 69-year-old actor, best known for playing Liz...

item.title: Supreme Court commends high voter turnout and peaceful polling in West Bengal elections
item.description: The Supreme Court of India appreciated the high voter turnout of over 92% recorded during the first phase of the West Bengal assembly elections, observing that active participation by citizens strengt...

item.title: Rohan Lund Appointed CEO of Newly Merged Southern Cross Media Group
item.description: Rohan Lund, former chief executive of NRMA, has been appointed to lead Southern Cross Media, the newly formed conglomerate that brings together Network Seven, the Triple M and Hit radio networks, and ...

item.title: Kigoma residents raise concerns over delays in Katosho dry port project and demand government action
item.description: Residents of Kigoma Municipality are now pressing the government to give clear direction and take serious steps on the long-delayed Katosho Dry Port project. This project, which started in 2019, was m...

item.title: Carolina takes commanding 3-0 series lead over Ottawa as Sanderson exits with injury
item.description: The Carolina Hurricanes moved to the brink of advancing in their playoff series against the Ottawa Senators with a decisive victory that gave them a 3-0 lead. The game underscored Carolina’s depth and...

item.title: Anne Hathaway’s Use of 'Inshallah' Sparks Global Reaction
item.description: Anne Hathaway recently used the Arabic term 'inshallah' during a global press tour, which quickly went viral. The phrase, meaning 'if God wills' or 'God willing', is common among Muslims and other Ara...

item.title: Newly Discovered Dinosaur Tracks in South Africa Shed Light on Cretaceous Life
item.description: A recent discovery of dinosaur tracks along the coast of South Africa’s Western Cape is rewriting part of the region's fossil history. For years, the Karoo Basin was considered a hotspot for ancient l...

item.title: PNP reports 287 gas stations closed and 77 arrests over fuel-related violations
item.description: The Philippine National Police (PNP) reported that as of April 24, 2026, a total of 287 gasoline stations across the country have been closed due to fuel-related violations, while 77 individuals have ...

item.title: Dangote Refinery: Boosting Africa’s Energy Independence and Unity
item.description: Africa is experiencing a major milestone with the Dangote Refinery now operating at full capacity, processing around 650,000 barrels of oil per day. This refinery is already exporting refined fuel to ...

item.title: Agnes Kagure Calls for Practical Measures to Help Families Cope with High Living Costs
item.description: Nairobi-based businesswoman and 2027 gubernatorial hopeful, Agnes Kagure, has raised concerns over Kenya's rising cost of living. She highlighted the recent fuel price hike by EPRA, which has led to i...

item.title: AFL: Emotional Anzac Ceremony and Tigers’ Big Match Upset
item.description: The Richmond Tigers and Melbourne Demons faced off at the MCG on Anzac Eve in what promised to be an emotional and thrilling clash. The pre-game Anzac ceremony was a standout, with the crowd immersed ...

item.title: Nigerian Military Begins Court Martial for 36 Accused in Alleged Coup Plot
item.description: The Nigerian military has launched a court martial to try 36 personnel accused of attempting to overthrow President Bola Tinubu's administration. The court proceedings were held in secrecy at the Scor...
